prevalence.value: ~0.3-1% (workforce); ~10-15% of adult asthma attributable to work exposures
prevalence.source: Occupational asthma epidemiology syntheses; WHO occupational risk context
prevalence.trend: stable; underdiagnosed due to limited occupational health evaluation

incidence.source: Occupational cohort studies and surveillance reports

burden.dalyPerCapita: low overall; significant productivity loss and chronic asthma burden in affected workers
burden.mortalityRate: Low; severe uncontrolled asthma risk exists

riskFactors: poor ventilation and lack of PPE
vulnerablePopulations: informal workers without occupational health coverage
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Asthma symptoms related to work with improvement off work; objective evidence via spirometry/peak flow variability across work-rest cycles; consider specific IgE for sensitizers and specialist evaluation

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: limited; occupational surveillance varies by employer and sector
publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Hundreds of thousands (subset of adult asthma)
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; best with exposure reduction/removal plus guideline-based asthma therapy

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Primary care recognition often limited; pulmonology/occupational medicine needed for confirmation and work-related assessment
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Reduce/remove exposure, workplace controls and PPE; ICS-based therapy per asthma severity; worker compensation/occupational reporting when applicable
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Underdiagnosis, informal labor market barriers, limited access to occupational medicine services
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: High-impact prevention when exposures are controlled; standard asthma medications broadly available
